title: The Absolute at Large
description: The Absolute at Large — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A Wiki of Karel Čapek's satirical science fiction novel The Absolute at Large (1922)
'Table of Contents'

memes.label: All Characters
memes.description: Complete index of all characters appearing in The Absolute at Large, sorted alphabetically. Dynamically rendered from person-type pages.

memes.label: All Concepts
memes.description: Complete index of all concepts, ideas, and themes explored in The Absolute at Large, sorted alphabetically. Dynamically rendered from concept-type pages.

memes.label: All Events
memes.description: Complete index of all significant events in The Absolute at Large, sorted alphabetically. Dynamically rendered from event-type pages.

memes.label: All Organizations
memes.description: Complete index of all organizations, institutions, and groups in The Absolute at Large, sorted alphabetically. Dynamically rendered from organization-type pages.

memes.label: All Places
memes.description: Complete index of all locations and places appearing in The Absolute at Large, sorted alphabetically. Dynamically rendered from place-type pages.

memes.label: All Works
memes.description: Complete index of all written works, publications, and documents referenced in The Absolute at Large, sorted alphabetically. Dynamically rendered from work-type pages.

memes.label: Atomic Energy
memes.description: The complete utilization of atomic energy through matter disintegration, the scientific principle behind the Karburator, presented as a revolutionary advance over conventional combustion.

memes.label: "Baron Yanato"
memes.description: "The Japanese Ambassador to the Supreme Council of the Great Powers, convened on the remote island of St. Kilda to address the global crisis caused by the Absolute. Baron Yanato's serene insistence that the Absolute 'is a great Japanese' — polite, smiling, and implacable — makes him Čapek's satirical portrait of nationalist appropriation of the divine. He delivers the novel's most economically devastating critique of religious imperialism with a smile that never wavers."

memes.label: "Battle at Gorochovky"
memes.description: "A battle of the Greatest War recorded by the historian Usinski in Čapek's The Absolute at Large — one of several final engagements whose competing claims to be 'the last battle' illustrate the collapse of historical certainty in the war's chaotic aftermath."

memes.label: "Battle at Sandberk"
memes.description: "A domestic military clash in Prague during the Absolute crisis in Čapek's The Absolute at Large — the Vršovice garrison, having adopted its own dogma of the Three Degrees of Salvation, issues an ultimatum to the Černin barracks and barricades itself in, triggering a siege by the Seventh Dragoons and Castle Guard."

memes.label: "Bishop Linda"
memes.description: "The Suffragan Bishop of the Roman Catholic Church who attempts to negotiate with G. H. Bondy for ecclesiastical control of the Absolute emanating from Marek's Břevnov cellar. A shrewd, pragmatic church politician who recognizes the Absolute as an opportunity for the Church to reclaim its spiritual monopoly, only to discover that the divine cannot be franchised."

memes.label: Board of Directors (M.E.C.)
memes.description: The governing board of the Metallo-Electrical Company (M.E.C.) in *The Absolute at Large*. The Board — presided over by an unnamed Chairman — oversees the company's rapid expansion of Karburator production in Chapter X, approving the construction of thousands of atomic boilers with full knowledge that each machine releases the Absolute as an uncontrollable byproduct.

memes.label: Bobinet's Coup
memes.description: Lieutenant Toni Bobinet's military coup against the Absolute — deposing his captain, declaring war on the Absolute, destroying Karburators across France, and ultimately crowning himself Emperor of the French.

memes.label: Bohemia
memes.description: The western and largest historical region of the Czech lands, forming the heartland of Czechoslovakia. In *The Absolute at Large*, Bohemia is the geographic stage for nearly all the novel's action — from Prague's Karburator-powered transformation to the rural spiritual movements along the Vltava and the regional conflicts of the apocalyptic Greatest War.

memes.label: "Břevnov"
memes.description: "The Břevnov district of Prague — specifically R. Marek's electric lamp factory at 1651 Mixa Street — is the ground zero of the Absolute's release into the world. The cellar beneath this modest factory houses the original Karburator and becomes the novel's first miraculous shrine, drawing religious pilgrims before the authorities seal off the entire street with barbed wire."

memes.label: Brno
memes.description: The capital of Moravia and the second-largest city of Czechoslovakia. In *The Absolute at Large*, Brno is cited as an early Karburator testing site — the Technical Institute's laboratory houses Karburator No. 3 — and later as a center of the Absolute's industrial takeover at the Brno cotton works.

memes.label: Brno Technical Institute
memes.description: The engineering institute in Brno where both Rudy Marek and G. H. Bondy studied, and where Karburator No. 3 was installed for testing. The Institute represents the scientific-technical establishment in *The Absolute at Large* — an institution that evaluates the Karburator purely on engineering merit while remaining oblivious to its metaphysical consequences.

memes.label: Canonization of the Absolute
memes.description: The Catholic Church's expedited process to declare the Absolute a God, culminating in a ceremony at St. Peter's on December 12th, as chronicled in Chapter XIX of the novel.

memes.label: Captain Trouble
memes.description: A tough, gun-toting sea captain who accompanies G.H. Bondy on his Pacific island refuge during the Greatest War, serving as a protective and bluntly pragmatic companion.

memes.label: Cardinal Varesi
memes.description: The Cardinal Archbishop of Venice appointed as Procurator Dei (God's Advocate) for the Absolute's canonization and deification process, who presented seventeen thousand testimonies of miracles to the Holy See.

memes.label: Church
memes.description: The institutional Church's confrontation with the Absolute — a satirical portrayal of organized religion's attempt to control, contain, and ultimately co-opt a genuine divine manifestation in Čapek's novel.

memes.label: City Council (Prague)
memes.description: The municipal governing body of Prague. In *The Absolute at Large*, the City Council is prominently involved in the ceremonial opening of the Central Karburator Power Station at Stvanice and later becomes a forum for the religious and political tensions unleashed by the Absolute's influence across the metropolis.

memes.label: "Conference of the Supreme Council"
memes.description: "A secret summit of world powers on the remote island of St. Kilda in Čapek's The Absolute at Large (Chapter XX), where diplomats gather to formulate a common policy toward the Absolute — only to discover that each nation's God has become a nationalist, and no agreement is possible."
aliases: Supreme Council of the Great Powers

memes.label: The Consistory
memes.description: The governing council of the Catholic Church in Prague. In *The Absolute at Large*, the Consistory negotiates with G. H. Bondy to place the Absolute under Church patronage, proposing to declare the Břevnov cellar a miraculous shrine and place of pilgrimage — an attempt by the institutional Church to co-opt and control the divine force emanating from Marek's Karburator.

memes.label: "Control of the Absolute"
memes.description: "The competition among Industry, Church, and State to control the Absolute — and why all three institutions fail, revealing Čapek's satirical thesis that no human institution can contain the divine."

memes.label: Coral Island (Hereheretua)
memes.description: A remote coral island in the Pacific, purchased by G. H. Bondy just before the outbreak of the Greatest War. The island — named Hereheretua — serves as Bondy's final refuge in *The Absolute at Large*'s concluding chapter, where the novel's central character confronts the ultimate consequences of the technology he brought to the world.

memes.label: Crusaders
memes.description: The Catholic military force formed during the religious wars of *The Absolute at Large*. The Crusaders fight against the Protestant Union in the great Central European conflict, representing one side of the sectarian violence that the Karburator's Absolute unleashes across the continent — a dark echo of the historical Crusades, now fought with atomic-powered weapons.

memes.label: Cyril Keval
memes.description: District reporter for the Prague *People's Journal* who covers the ceremonial opening of the Central Karburator Power Station at Stvanice and serves as the novel's journalistic eyewitness to the Absolute crisis. Through his eyes, the reader encounters the Carborundum factory and the broader social effects of the Absolute's release. (011-005)

memes.label: Czechoslovak Press Bureau
memes.description: The official state news agency of Czechoslovakia in *The Absolute at Large*, responsible for issuing government communiqués that sanitize — and occasionally deny — the chaotic events surrounding the Absolute phenomenon. The Press Bureau appears as a recurring background presence: the institution through which the state manages public perception of a reality it cannot control.

memes.label: Dejvice
memes.description: An upscale residential district of Prague, home to the Minister for War's villa. In *The Absolute at Large*, Dejvice becomes the site of the first high-level political casualty of the Absolute's influence, when the Minister experiences a religious awakening that leads to his public call for pacifism and martyrdom — and his immediate resignation.

memes.label: Demon
memes.description: The concept of demonic or Satanic forces as invoked by various parties in the novel to explain and oppose the Absolute's effects — revealing how established institutions frame disruptive spiritual phenomena as evil.

memes.label: Diplomatic Corps
memes.description: The body of foreign ambassadors and diplomats in *The Absolute at Large* who attend the Karburator opening ceremony and later convene the Supreme Council of the Great Powers on St. Kilda. The Diplomatic Corps represents the international political order that the Absolute phenomenon destabilizes — diplomats who are trained to negotiate human conflicts find themselves utterly unequipped to deal with a divine intervention.

memes.label: Divine Manifestation
memes.description: The physical manifestation of the divine Absolute when released from matter, as theorized through Spinoza, Fechner, and Leibniz.

memes.label: Doctrine
memes.description: Religious doctrine as a contested battleground in Čapek's novel — the Absolute's manifestations trigger fierce theological disputes as competing institutions race to define orthodoxy around the new divine presence.

memes.label: "Dr. Blahous"
memes.description: "A fifty-five-year-old Lecturer in Comparative Religion at the University of Prague who attempts to analyze the Absolute phenomenon with scholarly detachment — only to be touched by the divine at 4:30 a.m. while leaning out his window. His article, begun as a dismissal of religious mania as 'barbaric atavism,' transforms mid-composition into a prophetic call to embrace the Absolute. Čapek uses Blahous to satirize academic pretension, the self-interest of career scholars, and the inadequacy of rational discourse in the face of genuine transcendence."

memes.label: Dr. Hubka
memes.description: Leader of the opposition in the Czechoslovak parliament who questions G. H. Bondy's industrial plans and represents political skepticism toward the Carborundum enterprise.

memes.label: Dr. Wurm
memes.description: The Imperial German Chancellor who participates in international diplomatic negotiations concerning the Absolute crisis, representing German interests during the escalating global tensions.

memes.label: Ellen Machat
memes.description: Daughter of the wealthy landlord Mr. Machat and heiress to seventy million. After exposure to the Absolute, she develops the ability to read thoughts and dedicates herself to charitable work among the poor. Love interest of G. H. Bondy.

memes.label: Emperor Bobinet
memes.description: Self-proclaimed "Napoleon of the Mountain Brigade," a twenty-two-year-old lieutenant of mountain artillery stationed at Annecy (Haute Savoie) who — bored, isolated from the Absolute's influence in the high Alps, and inspired by a newspaper editorial — deposes his captain and launches a military campaign that makes him First Consul and then Emperor of France. (026-001)

memes.label: Faith
memes.description: Religious faith as transformed and democratized by the Absolute in Čapek's novel — a universal experience that bypasses institutional authority and manifests in primitive, ecstatic forms.

memes.label: "Father Jost"
memes.description: "A Catholic priest and editorial writer for the newspaper *The People's Friend*, whose well-meaning but incompetent polemics against the Absolute draw the wrath of Bishop Linda. Jost represents the Church's rank-and-file intellectuals — earnest, formulaic, and utterly unprepared for genuine divine manifestation. By the novel's end he has mellowed into Canon Jost, a supper-table philosopher debating sauerkraut preparation and the nature of religious belief."

memes.label: Father Novaček
memes.description: A Catholic priest who performs miraculous healings in Tyl Square and organizes the Maccabean armed guard during the religious upheavals, defying the Archbishop's prohibition on miracle-working.

memes.label: Free Thought Society
memes.description: A secularist organization in *The Absolute at Large* that holds public services in Havliček Square, Prague, amid the religious epidemic unleashed by the Absolute. The Free Thought Society represents the rationalist, anti-clerical countercurrent to the wave of religious enthusiasm — yet even this bastion of secularism cannot remain untouched by the Absolute's transformative power.

memes.label: "G. H. Bondy"
memes.description: "G. H. Bondy, Chairman of the Board of Directors of the Metallo-Electrical Company (M.E.C.), the pragmatic industrialist protagonist whose decision to commercialize the Karburator sets the novel's events in motion — a thirty-year veteran of industrial leadership whose transformation from crisis-haunted businessman to reluctant agent of global catastrophe embodies Capek's satire of capitalism, science, and religion."

memes.label: General Buchtin
memes.description: Imperial Russian General who participates in international diplomatic negotiations during the Absolute crisis, representing Russian military and political interests.

memes.label: "General Hampl"
memes.description: "A poor tailor and adventurer from Hradec Králové who, having served with the Foreign Legion and marched with Emperor Bobinet's troops, returns to his native city and stages a comic-opera coup d'état by self-promotion to 'General.' Čapek uses Hampl to satirize the theatrical nature of military power and the ease with which authority can be seized in times of chaos."

memes.label: God
memes.description: "The theological concept of God as a physical reality in Capek's novel — the Karburator's complete combustion of matter releases God as an industrial byproduct, transforming a metaphysical question into a technological crisis."

memes.label: Grace
memes.description: Divine grace as an immediate, transformative experience distributed by the Absolute — unmediated by sacraments and bestowed universally upon the worthy and unworthy alike.

memes.label: Grandfather Blahous
memes.description: Elderly patriarch of the Blahous family, residing in Seven Cottages (Sedm Chalup) during the final chapters of the novel. Father of Dr. Blahous, the university lecturer.

memes.label: The Hammer and Star (Masonic Lodge)
memes.description: A French-affiliated Masonic lodge in Prague, of which G. H. Bondy serves as Worshipful Master. In Chapter XVII of *The Absolute at Large*, the lodge convenes to debate the theological and moral implications of the Absolute, ultimately voting to declare neutrality — a moment of institutional evasion that mirrors the broader societal failure to confront the Karburator's consequences.

memes.label: Hampl's Declaration of War
memes.description: Hampl the tailor's imitation of Bobinet's coup in Hradec Králové — a satirical miniature of military insurrection in which a declaration of war, complete with drums and ultimatums, conquers an already-abandoned city.

memes.label: Heaven
memes.description: The concept of heaven and paradise in Čapek's novel — both a mundane exclamation and a transformative vision of earthly paradise created by the Absolute's influence on human society.

memes.label: Heresy
memes.description: Theological disputes over whether the Absolute constitutes orthodox divinity or heretical deviation, leading to schisms and doctrinal conflicts.

memes.label: Holiness
memes.description: A state of spiritual purity and moral transformation induced by the Absolute — a democratized holiness that bypasses institutional sanctification and manifests in ordinary individuals.

memes.label: Hradec Králové
memes.description: A city in eastern Bohemia, historically known as Königgrätz. In *The Absolute at Large*, Hradec Králové becomes one of the earliest municipalities to adopt Karburator technology, with its lighting system powered by a ten-kilo atomic boiler — a showcase of the M.E.C.'s rapid industrial expansion.

memes.label: Industrial Bank
memes.description: A Prague financial institution in *The Absolute at Large* that becomes an early casualty of the Absolute's influence. After a Karburator is installed in the bank's premises, its officials declare the bank's assets a "sacred trust for the poor" and go on religious strike — one of the first documented cases of the Absolute disrupting the financial system.

memes.label: "Invention of the Karburator"
memes.description: "The foundational event of Capek's novel: engineer R. Marek perfects the Karburator — a device achieving complete atomic combustion — and demonstrates it to his old schoolfellow G. H. Bondy, who experiences the divine Absolute for the first time in the Břevnov cellar. The meeting of inventor and industrialist sets in motion the global cascade of spiritual and social catastrophe that drives the novel."

memes.label: "Jan Binder Kuzenda"
memes.description: "Former political activist turned religious prophet on the Vltava dredge — a central figure in Capek's satire, whose levitation and miracles demonstrate the Absolute's democratizing effect on spirituality."

memes.label: M.E.C.
memes.description: "The Metallo-Electrical Company (M.E.C.), an industrial conglomerate led by G. H. Bondy, is the corporate engine that mass-produces the Karburator and inadvertently releases the Absolute upon the world. Operating ten factories with 34,000 employees, the M.E.C. is the leading producer of iron, boilers, and grates before pivoting to atomic energy — a decision that triggers global religious mania and economic catastrophe."

memes.label: Marshal Jollivet
memes.description: A French military officer serving under Emperor Bobinet during the Greatest War, featured in anecdotes highlighting Bobinet's supposed filial devotion and patriotism.

memes.label: The Masonic Lodge Meeting
memes.description: G. H. Bondy's extraordinary meeting of the "Hammer and Star" Masonic Lodge to address Clerical attacks linking Freemasonry to the Absolute's manifestations — a satirical portrayal of institutional indecision and self-interest.

memes.label: Masonic Lodges
memes.description: The Masonic Lodges in *The Absolute at Large* — most prominently the Free French Masonic Lodge "The Hammer and Star," whose Worshipful Master is G. H. Bondy. The Lodges become entangled in the Absolute crisis when the Clerical party accuses them of deliberately unleashing demoniacal powers, prompting the Grand Lodge to issue a decree directing Masters to organize religious circles in a Masonic spirit.

memes.label: Miracle
memes.description: Supernatural events produced by the release of the Absolute from matter through the Karburator — from personal healings to battlefield interventions, treated by Čapek as the inevitable industrial byproduct of atomic combustion.

memes.label: Monist Association
memes.description: A philosophical and religious organization in *The Absolute at Large* that claims the Absolute as scientific confirmation of Monist doctrine — the belief that all reality is a single unified substance, which Monists identify with God. The Association issues communications asserting theological ownership of the Absolute and, in Leipzig, lays the foundation-stone of the future Cathedral of the Atomic God.

memes.label: Monsignor Cullatti
memes.description: Papal Secretary to the Pope during the Absolute crisis. Served as Advocatus Diaboli (Devil's Advocate) in the expedited canonization and deification process of the Absolute.

memes.label: Moravia
memes.description: The eastern historical region of the Czech lands, adjoining Bohemia. In *The Absolute at Large*, Moravia appears in the novel's apocalyptic epilogue as a region whose "stubborn Catholics" resist foreign occupations and whose faithful population endures through successive waves of conquest.

memes.label: Mountain Brigade
memes.description: A military unit mentioned in *The Absolute at Large* whose members become converts to the Absolute during the religious epidemic. The Mountain Brigade exemplifies the Absolute's penetration of military institutions, transforming soldiers — whose professional identity is built on violence — into religious devotees seeking transcendence.

memes.label: Mr. Brych
memes.description: The stoker aboard Kuzenda's dredge M.E.28 on the Vltava River, an enthusiastic lay preacher who welcomes visitors to the floating "iron church" and becomes one of the earliest grassroots participants in the Absolute-inspired religious movement. (010-010)

memes.label: Mr. Hudec
memes.description: A visitor to Kuzenda's dredge who comes out of curiosity and becomes drawn into the Absolute-inspired religious services.

memes.label: Mr. Rejzek
memes.description: A newspaper editor at the Czechoslovak Press Bureau who processes international reports and discusses the strange effects of the Absolute with reporter Cyril Keval.

memes.label: Mrs. Blahous
memes.description: An elderly woman living in Seven Cottages, wife of Grandfather Blahous, who observes the chaos of the Greatest War from her rural home while continuing her daily domestic routines.

memes.label: Mrs. Prouzova
memes.description: An elderly country woman and neighbour of the Blahous family in Seven Cottages, who ponders the meaning of the Greatest War while leaning on her fence and yawning at the mysteries of the world.

memes.label: Omnipotence
memes.description: The Absolute's attribute of unlimited power, which manifests chaotically through industrial overproduction and economic collapse rather than through organized divine action.

memes.label: Pacific Ocean
memes.description: The Pacific Ocean appears in *The Absolute at Large* as the distant theater of the global Karburator-driven religious wars. The conflict between Japan and China that eventually spills into Central Europe originates in the Pacific, and the ocean becomes a symbol of the worldwide reach of the Absolute's influence.

memes.label: Pantheism
memes.description: "Pantheism — the belief that God or the Absolute is manifest in everything that exists — serves as the theological framework through which R. Marek explains the Karburator's side-effect to G. H. Bondy in Chapter III, and recurs throughout the novel as the default religious position of the scientifically minded, ultimately weaponized by both the Catholic Church (which condemns it as heresy) and secular critics (who dismiss the Absolute phenomenon as atavistic pantheism)."

memes.label: Petřín
memes.description: A hill in Prague, site of a wireless telegraph station. In *The Absolute at Large*, the Petřín wireless station becomes a notable hotspot of the Absolute's religious epidemic, with operators on duty spontaneously transmitting ecstatic gospel messages to the world — an early sign of the Karburator's effects spreading through communication infrastructure.

memes.label: The Munich Popular Assembly
memes.description: The Munich Popular Assembly of the Greatest War era — a mass gathering that passed a resolution for Southern German secession and triggered the chain of military escalations that launched the Holy War on the Lutherans.

memes.label: Prague
memes.description: The capital city of Czechoslovakia and the primary setting of Karel Capek's *The Absolute at Large*. Prague serves as the epicenter of the [[the-karburator|Karburator]] revolution, from its industrial deployment in Břevnov to its catastrophic social and spiritual consequences, culminating in the destruction of the [[charles-bridge|Charles Bridge]] itself (009-020).

memes.label: "Prague in the Novel"
memes.description: "Prague serves as the primary urban setting of Capek's The Absolute at Large — the city where the Karburator is invented in Břevnov, mass-produced at Vysočany, and celebrated (disastrously) at the Stvanice power station. As the Absolute spreads, Prague's districts, landmarks, and institutions become the first casualties of the manufactured divine, transforming the Czech capital from industrial hub into a laboratory of religious chaos."

memes.label: "Prince Trivelino"
memes.description: "The Italian Minister and delegate to the secret St. Kilda Conference of the Great Powers. Prince Trivelino represents Italy's complex religious position — caught between the Vatican and the Quirinal, between Catholic universalism and nationalist particularism — in Čapek's satire of how the Absolute fractures international diplomacy along national and confessional lines."

memes.label: Protestant Union
memes.description: Military-political coalition of Protestant states formed at Stettin during the religious wars sparked by the Absolute. Opposed the Catholic Crusaders in the devastating pan-European conflict.

memes.label: Reason
memes.description: Human reason as a faculty rendered inadequate by the Absolute — Čapek's novel systematically demonstrates reason's failure to comprehend, resist, or contain the divine force unleashed by the Karburator.

memes.label: Religious Conversion
memes.description: The sudden religious transformations experienced by individuals exposed to the Absolute, ranging from quiet illumination to dramatic conversions and apostle-like zeal.

memes.label: Religious Cults
memes.description: The various religious sects, movements, and worship communities that emerged in response to the Absolute's manifestations through widespread Karburator use.

memes.label: Rosenthal
memes.description: A business associate of G. H. Bondy who is reported to have "gone crazy" under the influence of the Absolute, illustrating the disruptive effect of religious awakening on the business world.

memes.label: "Rudolf Marek"
memes.description: "The inventor of the Karburator — a complete-atomic-combustion engine that releases the Absolute as a byproduct. A former classmate of G. H. Bondy at the Technical School, Marek is a brilliant but conscience-stricken engineer who spends twenty years creating the greatest invention in history, only to discover it unleashes God upon the world."

memes.label: Sacred Congregation
memes.description: The Vatican body — likely the Sacred Congregation of Rites — that formally admits the Absolute into the bosom of the Catholic Church in *The Absolute at Large*. The Sacred Congregation takes cognizance of petitions from the faithful and issues the canonical ruling that paves the way for the Absolute's ecclesiastical recognition, treating the divine industrial byproduct as a matter of bureaucratic procedure.

memes.label: Salvation Army
memes.description: The charitable Christian organization briefly mentioned in *The Absolute at Large* as one of several institutions — alongside the Free Thought Society, the Theosophical Centre "Adyar," and the Masonic Lodges — that publicly endorsed or engaged with the Absolute phenomenon during the height of the religious excitement.

memes.label: Science
memes.description: Science as both the origin and the victim of the Absolute — Marek's scientific discovery of atomic decomposition unleashes a force that renders scientific materialism untenable.

memes.label: Seven Cottages
memes.description: A small settlement mentioned in *The Absolute at Large* as the location where the gamekeeper Rys (a follower of Kuzenda's dredge community) resides. The name evokes the rural, agrarian Bohemian landscape that provides the backdrop for the novel's grassroots spiritual movements.

memes.label: Sin
memes.description: The concept of sin as transformed by the Absolute's influence — traditional moral transgressions reframed through the lens of ecstatic religious experience in Čapek's satirical novel.

memes.label: Slaný
memes.description: A town in Central Bohemia, one of the earliest sites of Karburator deployment in *The Absolute at Large*. A five-kilo pattern Karburator (No. 6) was installed to run a mill at Slaný, making it part of the M.E.C.'s initial testing program before the Absolute's effects became publicly known.

memes.label: Soul
memes.description: The human soul as the site of the Absolute's most intimate effects — the inner faculty through which individuals experience divine presence, religious conversion, and moral transformation in Čapek's novel.

memes.label: Spirit
memes.description: The concept of spirit — the immaterial divine substance — as articulated through Spinozan pantheism and manifested through the Absolute's effects on human consciousness.

memes.label: Spread of the Absolute
memes.description: The geographic and temporal trajectory by which the Absolute, released from Marek's Karburator, spreads from a single cellar in Břevnov to become a worldwide phenomenon — following a pattern of laboratory to capital to waterways to global dissemination, with industrial infrastructure serving as the arteries of the divine.

memes.label: St. Kilda
memes.description: A remote Scottish island in the North Atlantic, the title setting of Chapter XX of *The Absolute at Large*. St. Kilda represents the farthest geographic reach of the Absolute's influence, as even this isolated Hebridean outpost — the most remote inhabited place in the British Isles — is not beyond the reach of the Karburator's spiritual consequences.

memes.label: Štěchovice
memes.description: A town on the Vltava River in central Bohemia, near the site of the M.E.28 dredge where the religious community led by Mr. Kuzenda gathered. Štěchovice becomes a pilgrimage destination and the epicenter of the "Kuzendist" spiritual movement in *The Absolute at Large*.

memes.label: Stvanice
memes.description: An island in the Vltava River in Prague, site of the Central Karburator Electric Power Station for Greater Prague. The ceremonial opening of the fifty-kilo Karburator at Stvanice is a pivotal set-piece in The Absolute at Large, marking the moment when the Absolute's effects first erupt into public consciousness.

memes.label: Sugar Bank
memes.description: A Prague bank in *The Absolute at Large* that becomes the first financial institution to install a Karburator for central heating. The Absolute's influence causes its officials to give away money and burn banknotes in an incident labeled "Religious Bolshevism," leading to the bank's closure by police.

memes.label: Supernatural Phenomena
memes.description: The various supernatural manifestations — levitations, prophecies, miracle cures, and battlefield interventions — caused by the Absolute's release from matter through the Karburator.

memes.label: "The Absolute"
memes.description: "The divine essence released as a byproduct of the Karburator's complete atomic combustion — the central metaphysical concept of Capek's satirical novel, representing God in chemically pure, industrially manufactured form."

memes.label: "The Battle of Hradec Králové"
memes.description: "The climactic battle in Capek's The Absolute at Large (Chapter XXVI), where the religious fervor unleashed by the Absolute culminates in the so-called Greatest War — the apocalyptic world conflict that ends civilization, rendered through the satirical microcosm of a single Bohemian town."

memes.label: "The Board Meeting"
memes.description: "The fateful February 20th board meeting of the Metallo-Electrical Company in Čapek's The Absolute at Large (Chapter VI), where G. H. Bondy faces a shareholder rebellion over the Karburator's mysterious side-effects — and where the first signs of the Absolute's influence on the business elite become impossible to ignore."

memes.label: "The Catholic Church"
memes.description: "The institutional Catholic Church hierarchy in Capek's satirical novel, which initially rejects the Absolute as heretical pantheism, then pragmatically absorbs and canonizes it — embodying the novel's critique of how organized religion manages, confines, and ultimately commodifies the divine."

memes.label: "The Ceremony at Stvanice"
memes.description: "The ceremonial opening of the Central Karburator Electric Power Station on Stvanice Island in Čapek's The Absolute at Large (Chapter IX) — a satirical set-piece where the official speeches drone on for hours, the Absolute overwhelms the assembled dignitaries with religious ecstasy, and the press bureau issues an exquisitely evasive denial."
aliases: Opening of the Central Karburator Power Station

memes.label: The Deification Ceremony
memes.description: The Catholic Church's formal canonization and deification ceremony of the Absolute in Rome, marking the theological climax of the Absolute's integration into organized religion.

memes.label: "The First Blow Struck"
memes.description: "The first violent clash between rival religious factions in Čapek's The Absolute at Large (Chapter XI) — a brawl between Jan Binder's merry-go-round congregation and Kuzenda's dredge worshippers at Stechovice, marking the moment the Absolute's influence turns from personal illumination to collective violence."

memes.label: "The Greatest War"
memes.description: "The apocalyptic global religious war (February 12, 1944 to autumn 1953) in Capek's The Absolute at Large, fought between rival sects and empires after the Absolute's release — a satirical depiction of humanity's tendency to turn every belief into a cause for mass slaughter, claiming 198 million lives with only thirteen survivors. (025-003)"

memes.label: "The Holy War"
memes.description: "The global religious war triggered by the Absolute in Čapek's The Absolute at Large — a satirical cascade of holy wars, crusades, and sectarian conflicts that begins in Bavaria with a lead-pencil factory brawl and ends in the apocalyptic Greatest War, claiming 198 million lives."

memes.label: "The Karburator"
memes.description: "R. Marek's revolutionary invention that achieves complete atomic combustion, releasing both unlimited mechanical energy and the divine Absolute as a byproduct — the technological catalyst of Čapek's novel." [[god]]

memes.label: "The Last Battle"
memes.description: "The final engagement of the Greatest War in Čapek's The Absolute at Large (Chapter XXIX), where the last thirteen soldiers in the world abandon the conflict and fall asleep under a birch tree — a comic epitaph to the apocalyptic religious war unleashed by the Absolute."
aliases: Last Battle of the Greatest War

memes.label: "The People's Journal"
memes.description: "The Prague newspaper that serves as both narrative lens and satirical mouthpiece in Capek's The Absolute at Large — a bustling newsroom where the staff report on the [[the-absolute|Absolute]]'s unraveling of the world while struggling to find anything 'natural' to report, embodying Capek's critique of journalism's entanglement with religious and political power."

memes.label: "The Process of Canonization"
memes.description: "The Catholic Church's formal canonization and deification of the Absolute in Čapek's The Absolute at Large (Chapter XIX) — a satirical depiction of institutional religion's attempt to co-opt the divine, complete with seventeen thousand miracle testimonies, self-serving scientific endorsements, and the declaration 'Habemus Deum' atop a twelve-ton Karburator installed behind the high altar of St. Peter's."

memes.label: Total Combustion
memes.description: The complete annihilation of matter by the Karburator, releasing the Absolute trapped within atoms and yielding immense energy.

memes.label: "Types of Believers"
memes.description: "A literary taxonomy of the six distinct religious response patterns to the Absolute in Čapek's novel, from reluctant prophet to rationalist observer — satirizing the human tendency to domesticate divine experience into familiar social roles."

memes.label: Vision
memes.description: Supernatural visions as one of the phenomena produced by the Absolute — prophetic and revelatory experiences that multiply globally as the Absolute's influence spreads, blending apocalyptic imagery with comic absurdity.

memes.label: Vltava River
memes.description: The principal river of Bohemia, flowing through Prague. In *The Absolute at Large*, the Vltava is the setting for key spiritual events — Kuzenda's sacred dredge floats on its waters, and its valley witnesses the convergence of technology, nature, and divine manifestation.

memes.label: Worship
memes.description: The spontaneous religious worship that erupts when people are exposed to the Absolute's emanations — a central mechanism through which Čapek explores mass religious psychology.
